📢 Call for Papers | IJMEER Volume 1, Issue 2 (October 2026)

The International Journal of Multidisciplinary Educational Excellence and Research (IJMEER) invites original research articles, review papers, and case studies for its upcoming October 2026 issue.

We welcome submissions across multidisciplinary fields:
• Law & Legal Studies
• Education & Pedagogy
• Management & Business Administration
• Social Sciences & Humanities
• Technology & Innovation
• Environmental Studies
• Public Policy & Governance

📅 Submission Deadline: August 31, 2026
📧 Submit at: ijmeerj@gmail.com
🌐 Author Guidelines: https://www.ijmeer.com/authors.html

IJMEER is a Diamond Open Access journal — free to read, free to publish. All submissions undergo rigorous double-blind peer review.

Published by Meer Foundation | Est. 2026

📖 Research Spotlight | From IJMEER Volume 1, Issue 1 (April 2026)

We are proud to highlight the outstanding research published in our inaugural issue. IJMEER's first volume featured peer-reviewed articles covering diverse academic disciplines — from legal scholarship to educational innovation.

Each article in Volume 1, Issue 1 represents the dedication of researchers committed to advancing multidisciplinary knowledge.

As we prepare for Volume 1, Issue 2 (October 2026), we invite new submissions from researchers worldwide.

3.linkedin:
🌟 Meet Our Editorial Team | Dr. Nusrat Ali Hashmi — Editor-in-Chief

We are honoured to introduce Dr. Nusrat Ali Hashmi, Editor-in-Chief of IJMEER and a distinguished Bombay High Court Advocate with over 20 years of experience in legal scholarship and academic leadership.

Dr. Hashmi's expertise in law, governance, and multidisciplinary research provides the editorial vision that drives IJMEER's commitment to rigorous, impactful scholarship.

Under her leadership, IJMEER launched its inaugural issue in April 2026, establishing a new platform for researchers across disciplines.

🔗 View the full editorial board: https://www.ijmeer.com/editorial-board.html
🔗 Detailed profiles: https://www.ijmeer.com/editorial-portfolio.html

3.facebook:
🌟 MEET OUR EDITOR-IN-CHIEF | Dr. Nusrat Ali Hashmi

IJMEER is honoured to be led by Dr. Nusrat Ali Hashmi, a distinguished Bombay High Court Advocate with over 20 years of experience in legal scholarship and academic leadership.

Dr. Hashmi's vision for IJMEER is clear: to create a platform where researchers from all disciplines can share their work freely, with rigour and integrity.

Key highlights:
✦ Bombay High Court Advocate
✦ 20+ years of legal and academic expertise
✦ Pioneer of IJMEER's Diamond Open Access mission
✦ Advocate for multidisciplinary scholarship

Under her leadership, IJMEER published its inaugural issue in April 2026 and continues to build a diverse, international editorial board.

🔗 View the full editorial board: www.ijmeer.com/editorial-board.html
🔗 Detailed profiles: www.ijmeer.com/editorial-portfolio.html

6.linkedin:
🌟 Honouring the Legacy of Ibn Sina (Avicenna) | 980–1037 CE

Ibn Sina, known in the West as Avicenna, was a Persian polymath whose contributions to medicine, philosophy, astronomy, and mathematics remain foundational to modern scholarship.

His magnum opus, "The Canon of Medicine" (Al-Qanun fi al-Tibb), served as a standard medical reference across Europe and the Islamic world for over six centuries — a testament to the power of multidisciplinary scholarship.

At IJMEER, we draw inspiration from scholars like Ibn Sina who bridged disciplines and advanced human knowledge across boundaries.

📖 Explore multidisciplinary research: https://www.ijmeer.com/archive.html

#IJMEER #IbnSina #Avicenna #HistoryOfScience #IslamicGoldenAge #MultidisciplinaryResearch #OpenAccess #AcademicHeritage #Medicine #Philosophy

6.facebook:
🌟 REMEMBERING IBN SINA (AVICENNA) | 980–1037 CE

Ibn Sina, known in the West as Avicenna, was one of the most influential polymaths in human history. His contributions spanned medicine, philosophy, astronomy, chemistry, and mathematics — the epitome of multidisciplinary scholarship.

Key Achievements:
📖 "The Canon of Medicine" (Al-Qanun fi al-Tibb) — used as the standard medical textbook in Europe and the Islamic world for over 600 years
🔬 Pioneered the concept of infectious diseases and quarantine
💡 Significant contributions to Aristotelian philosophy
📐 Advances in mathematics and natural sciences

Ibn Sina's legacy reminds us that the greatest breakthroughs come from crossing disciplinary boundaries — the very principle that drives IJMEER.

"The knowledge of anything, since all things have causes, is not acquired or complete unless it is known by its causes." — Ibn Sina

🌐 Explore multidisciplinary research at IJMEER: www.ijmeer.com

#IJMEER #IbnSina #Avicenna #HistoryOfScience #IslamicGoldenAge #MultidisciplinaryResearch #AcademicHeritage
